jokes.text: I'm so happy to see all of you and to welcome you to Season Four. Which is, of course, the name of this restaurant
jokes.setup: Standard corporate welcome to new season
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're at a restaurant called 'Season Four'
jokes.explanation: Classic 30 Rock misdirection - sets up corporate meeting expectation then subverts with absurd restaurant name coincidence

jokes.text: Cheesy Blasters jingle and Meat Cat flies away on his skateboard
jokes.setup: Introducing fake food product
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical mascot 'Meat Cat' on skateboard
jokes.explanation: Peak 30 Rock absurdism - the jingle is catchy and the Meat Cat concept is memorably ridiculous

jokes.text: All of America is America
jokes.setup: Jack talking about 'real America'
jokes.punchline: Liz's logical but obvious correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz Lemon character moment - cutting through corporate nonsense with blunt logic

jokes.text: Then how come I got sued for sexual harassment at it?
jokes.setup: Tracy defending bucket drumming as a real job
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was sexually harassing people while street performing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Tracy logic - escalates from defending his job to accidentally revealing inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: Don't look at me in the eyes!
jokes.setup: Tracy demonstrating how rich people eat lobster
jokes.punchline: Aggressive demand about eye contact while eating
jokes.explanation: Pure Tracy absurdism - random aggressive behavior that somehow makes sense for his character

jokes.text: I better talk to Rabbi Shmuley about this
jokes.setup: Tracy wondering if he's lost touch with his roots
jokes.punchline: Rich black man consulting a rabbi about authenticity
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail - Tracy's wealthy disconnect shown through consulting unlikely spiritual advisor

jokes.text: The best way for a lady to get heat in this industry is to either record a country album or have a lesbian relationship
jokes.setup: Jenna asking about 'going country'
jokes.punchline: Jack's cynical industry advice
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of Hollywood publicity strategies, delivered with Jack's characteristic bluntness

jokes.text: We'll trick those race-car-loving wideloads into watching your lefty, homoerotic propaganda hour yet
jokes.setup: Jack planning to appeal to broader audience
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest description of TGS and target audience
jokes.explanation: Peak Jack Donaghy - combining corporate strategy with shocking honesty and cultural stereotypes

jokes.text: Well, you just don't like anybody, do you?
jokes.setup: Jack's brutal assessment of audiences
jokes.punchline: Liz's defeated realization about Jack's worldview
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Liz processing Jack's cynicism with resigned humor

jokes.text: Uh, he's burning money again
jokes.setup: Discussion about Tracy not testing well
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of Tracy literally burning money
jokes.explanation: Perfect throwaway gag - Tracy's wealth excess treated as mundane workplace problem

jokes.text: Right, Josh, I forgot about that guy. You think that's a good sign?
jokes.setup: Liz asking about Josh
jokes.punchline: Jack immediately admitting he forgot Josh exists
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about forgotten cast member, works as both character moment and commentary

jokes.text: Our new mammogram machine is called the 'Git-Er-Done 2000'
jokes.setup: Every division reaching out to middle America
jokes.punchline: Medical equipment with redneck branding
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd corporate pandering - medical equipment with Larry the Cable Guy branding

jokes.text: Another actor? Why, they have so many feelings and opinions?
jokes.setup: Discussion of finding new cast member
jokes.punchline: Actor complaining about actors having feelings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy self-awareness - complaining about actor traits while being an actor

jokes.text: They never did find her earlobe
jokes.setup: Story about blonde intern wanting to be actress
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of violent outcome
jokes.explanation: Peak dark comedy - escalates from jealousy story to implied violence with perfect nonchalance

jokes.text: Pete's stealing money. Liz's uterus fell out.
jokes.setup: Trying to distract Kenneth with fake secrets
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd fake revelations
jokes.explanation: Good escalating absurdity as Liz panics and makes up increasingly ridiculous secrets

jokes.text: So naturally, you came to me because this company is just the two of us
jokes.setup: Kenneth coming to him about timecard problem
jokes.punchline: Jack's sarcastic response to being bothered with minor issue
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack sarcasm - corporate executive annoyed by having to deal with employee issues

jokes.text: As the Hill People say, 'Parcell gaw say del go up de saw say'
jokes.setup: Kenneth talking about Parcell family honesty
jokes.punchline: Incomprehensible hillbilly saying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth character moment - nonsensical folk wisdom delivered with complete sincerity

jokes.text: Today's my birthday
jokes.setup: Jack explaining budget cuts and envelope stuffing machine
jokes.punchline: Pete's sad, ignored birthday announcement
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - Pete's personal milestone ignored amid corporate talk

jokes.text: Dah don say da bay ton daw
jokes.setup: Kenneth responding to Jack's request to sign timecard
jokes.punchline: Another incomprehensible Hill People saying
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier Hill People saying, maintaining Kenneth's character consistency

jokes.text: Kenneth? Boo! Thank you, sir.
jokes.setup: Jack calling for Kenneth
jokes.punchline: Kenneth appearing suddenly and thanking Jack for scaring him
jokes.explanation: Kenneth's polite response to being startled fits his overly helpful character perfectly

jokes.text: Nothing. Uterus nothing.
jokes.setup: Jenna asking what Liz and Pete are whispering about
jokes.punchline: Liz accidentally referencing her earlier fake secret
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Liz's panic making her reference her own fake secret

jokes.text: You two have built a protective shell around me like a hermit crab or a mermaid boobie
jokes.setup: Tracy talking about being sheltered from common people
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical comparison to hermit crab and 'mermaid boobie'
jokes.explanation: Peak Tracy nonsense - starts with legitimate metaphor then veers into absurd 'mermaid boobie' territory

jokes.text: And then your wife starts getting all mad because the roof won't close and the bed that's in the shape of your face is getting rained on?
jokes.setup: Tracy trying to relate to common man about losing remote
jokes.punchline: Reveals his house has retractable roof and face-shaped bed
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - starts relatable then reveals absurd wealth through increasingly specific details

jokes.text: Can I feel the rough skin on your hands?
jokes.setup: Tracy trying to connect with working man
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate physical request that reveals his disconnect
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe - Tracy's attempt to understand working class becomes inappropriate and condescending

jokes.text: Um... which one is the elevator I'm not afraid of?
jokes.setup: Tracy declaring independence to go out on the street
jokes.punchline: Immediately needs help with basic building navigation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character beat - Tracy's declaration of independence immediately undermined by his helplessness

jokes.text: Hey, he's got a girl's middle name too
jokes.setup: Kenneth accidentally getting Jack's paycheck
jokes.punchline: Focusing on wrong detail - middle name instead of amount
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth logic - missing the important part (salary) to focus on irrelevant personal detail

jokes.text: I'm picking up my 'new tritionist' and his elderly son
jokes.setup: Liz making excuse about not sharing cab
jokes.punchline: Mispronunciation creates nonsense word
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay joke showing Liz's panic leading to verbal stumbles

jokes.text: All those zeros. It's downright un-American.
jokes.setup: Kenneth seeing Jack's huge paycheck
jokes.punchline: Calling excessive wealth un-American
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Kenneth's innocent patriotism clashing with capitalist reality

jokes.text: 'Bonus' means 'extra'. I know that from game shows.
jokes.setup: Kenneth learning about Jack's bonus check
jokes.punchline: Citing game shows as source of financial knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth innocence - learning complex financial concepts from TV game shows

jokes.text: That's where you're wrong, Kenneth. It's extremely American. My talents are more valuable than yours, so I'm paid accordingly.
jokes.setup: Kenneth calls Jack's bonus un-American
jokes.punchline: Jack's brutally honest defense of wealth inequality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack - his unapologetic embrace of capitalism and social hierarchy

jokes.text: I would like you to sign your name to a piece of paper that says, 'I am a big, ol' liar.'
jokes.setup: Kenneth demanding Jack admit to lying
jokes.punchline: Childish wording of formal demand
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth - formal negotiation demand using childish playground language

jokes.text: I am in the middle of a raging period! Of economic turmoil.
jokes.setup: Jack threatening Kenneth
jokes.punchline: Catch and redirect from menstrual reference to economic
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Jack catching himself mid-inappropriate metaphor and redirecting

jokes.text: What do we want? To get you sandwiches! When do we want it? Whenever will be convenient for you!
jokes.setup: Traditional strike chant format
jokes.punchline: Overly polite and accommodating demands
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of protest format - pages too nice to make real demands

jokes.text: Do you know the song 'Are You Ready For Some Football?' Do I? That's what my phone plays whenever Ray Lewis calls me.
jokes.setup: Jack asking if Jenna knows football song
jokes.punchline: Reveals personal connection to NFL player
jokes.explanation: Good Jack character moment - casual name-drop revealing high-level connections

jokes.text: What sports does N.B.C. have these days? Oh, off-season tennis.
jokes.setup: Jenna asking about NBC sports
jokes.punchline: Jack's deflating answer about their limited sports portfolio
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of excitement with reality of NBC's poor sports situation

jokes.text: Whatever religious undergarment Kenneth wears is in a twist
jokes.setup: Jack explaining Kenneth's problem with bonus
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'panties in a twist' with Kenneth's religious background
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay adapting common phrase to Kenneth's religious character

jokes.text: I think he's a friend. Uh... Oh, never mind. There's a door, I'm out. Oh, it's sunny!
jokes.setup: Tracy lost on the street calling for help
jokes.punchline: Confusion about basic outdoor experience
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy helplessness - even finding his way outside is an achievement requiring commentary

jokes.text: Are you a large child or a small adult? You look regular.
jokes.setup: Tracy trying to connect with random person
jokes.punchline: Bizarre questions revealing his disconnect from normal people
jokes.explanation: Peak Tracy absurdism - attempting normal conversation with completely abnormal questions

jokes.text: Do you have change for a $10,000 bill?
jokes.setup: Tracy continuing to try connecting with common people
jokes.punchline: Asking for change for non-existent massive bill
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy wealth disconnect - treating impossible denomination as normal currency

jokes.text: I would like some chicken nuggets, a beer, and some of my wife's rice, to stay.
jokes.setup: Tracy trying to order food
jokes.punchline: Bizarre order mixing restaurant items with personal possessions
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy confusion - not understanding how restaurants work, trying to order personal items

jokes.text: It's like I tell my assistant. Your weight is a reflection on me.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining how strike embarrasses company
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of body-shaming her assistant
jokes.explanation: Dark character moment showing Liz's capacity for casual cruelty beneath her liberal facade

jokes.text: How do you kill a snake? You cut off the head. Of course! Thank you. Now I won't be afraid to go into my garage.
jokes.setup: Liz giving metaphorical advice about handling strike
jokes.punchline: Jack taking it literally for actual snake problem
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - metaphor taken literally revealing Jack's separate problem

jokes.text: They used to call me the Chameleon. Because of my slender frame and my big, wet eyes.
jokes.setup: Liz claiming undercover experience with cool nickname
jokes.punchline: Reason is unflattering physical description, not stealth ability
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - cool spy nickname undermined by pathetic physical description

jokes.text: It's an L.L. Bean child's wallet from the 1970s. There's no money in it, but I was one hole-punch away from a free Tasti D-Lite.
jokes.setup: Liz looking for lost wallet
jokes.punchline: Pathetic details about children's wallet and minor loyalty program loss
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz patheticness - adult using child's wallet, devastated over minor reward loss

jokes.text: Yes, she will give it back to you when he goes home and gets it from her wife.
jokes.setup: Confusing pronoun usage about getting wallet back
jokes.punchline: Accidentally implying complex gender/relationship confusion
jokes.explanation: Mild confusion comedy from Pete's nervous pronoun scrambling

jokes.text: Yes, yes, we are doing it. No! So there you go, case closed. Pete and I are intercoursing each other.
jokes.setup: Jenna asking if Liz and Pete are having sex
jokes.punchline: Liz's contradictory responses and awkward euphemism
jokes.explanation: Good cringe - Liz's panic leading to contradictory statements and awkward word choice

jokes.text: Liz, I found your wallet in what our children call 'the big, wed cawr'.
jokes.setup: Paula calling about Liz's wallet
jokes.punchline: Baby talk pronunciation of 'red car'
jokes.explanation: Mild character comedy showing Paula's overly cutesy parental communication style

jokes.text: If this is what Pete needs, I would be willing to welcome you into our lovemaking.
jokes.setup: Paula thinking Pete and Liz are having affair
jokes.punchline: Offering threesome solution with clinical language
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe - overly understanding wife offering awkward solution in formal language

jokes.text: Pete, I know that I haven't been very sexual since my mother died.
jokes.setup: Paula explaining marriage problems
jokes.punchline: TMI about sexual dysfunction tied to death
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy TMI - inappropriate oversharing about intimate problems

jokes.text: I don't need this show, because I could get a job tomorrow in the Air Force!
jokes.setup: Josh quitting in anger
jokes.punchline: Threatening to join military as backup career plan
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - actor's dramatic exit undermined by unrealistic threat

jokes.text: That's incorrect! Lift with your legs!
jokes.setup: Josh struggles with the door while quitting
jokes.punchline: Tracy gives lifting advice while Josh fails at dramatic exit
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy deflation. Tracy's helpful advice during dramatic moment.

jokes.text: They are a blanket union that includes mall Santas, horse whisperers, and bucket drummers.
jokes.setup: Explaining Local 415 joining the strike
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd job categories in same union
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - starts reasonable with mall Santas, gets absurd with horse whisperers

jokes.text: I'm organizing a viral protest on 'Tweeter' and 'Youtubes'.
jokes.setup: Young page describing social media strategy
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing Twitter and YouTube
jokes.explanation: Mild generation gap humor - young person not quite understanding social media names

jokes.text: What show are you assigned to again? Donahue.
jokes.setup: Kenneth questioning Brandon's credentials
jokes.punchline: Revelation he works on long-cancelled show
jokes.explanation: Good absurd detail - page still assigned to show that ended decades ago

jokes.text: Meet my new friends, 'Nobody', and his wife, 'Susan Walters-Hyphen-Nobody'.
jokes.setup: Tracy introducing his new common people friends
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of 'nobody' as actual names
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay - Tracy's literal interpretation of 'nobody' as actual person names

jokes.text: Once I'm vested, I'm out of here.
jokes.setup: Bucket drummer joining Tracy's cause
jokes.punchline: Street performer talking about corporate benefits
jokes.explanation: Good irony - street performer with corporate mindset about benefits and career planning

jokes.text: ♪ It's tennis night in America ♪ ♪ Grab some buds and some brews, it's going to be a fight ♪
jokes.setup: Jenna records country song for NBC Sports
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous lyrics trying to make tennis sound exciting and American
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist country song making tennis sound like NASCAR

jokes.text: I came in here to shoot these tennis promos, and they had blue gels on the lights. You know that makes my teeth look see through.
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining why she's upset
jokes.punchline: Narcissistic focus on appearance over actual problems
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna self-absorption - missing real crisis to focus on dental aesthetics

jokes.text: If it is a blonde woman, I will kill myself!
jokes.setup: Learning about new cast member
jokes.punchline: Immediate threat of suicide over specific threat type
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna drama - immediately jumping to suicide threat over career competition

jokes.text: He was impervious to the charms of the 'Nympho Coed'. Charlene LaRue.
jokes.setup: Explaining Kenneth can't be corrupted
jokes.punchline: Specific mention of failed seductress with professional name
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - implies corporate espionage attempt with specifically named operative

jokes.text: Would you like something to eat? I have some leftover turtle meat from dinner. Or as you would call it, 'bonus' turtle meat.
jokes.setup: Kenneth offering food to Jack
jokes.punchline: References their earlier fight about bonuses while offering weird food
jokes.explanation: Good callback tying their conflict to Kenneth's weird food offerings

jokes.text: There's a bar in the shower that the previous tenant installed to keep from slipping. He still died in there, though.
jokes.setup: Jack asking for bar to make drink
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of death in apartment with safety equipment
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - safety equipment failure delivered with Kenneth's innocent tone

jokes.text: Those glasses are for display only.
jokes.setup: Jack going to make drink
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's fake glasses revelation
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - Kenneth's inexplicable decoration choices extend to fake barware

jokes.text: Sir, you sound like the mall Santas when they come back from lunch.
jokes.setup: Jack sounding dejected and philosophical
jokes.punchline: Comparison to drunk mall Santas
jokes.explanation: Good callback with new context - mall Santas as comparison point for drunken philosophy

jokes.text: That's the one where Tracy tore up that picture of the Pope. In his defense, it was Pope Innocent the Fourth. Because he increased taxation in the Papal States.
jokes.setup: Can't rerun controversial episode
jokes.punchline: Defending Tracy's actions with specific historical papal criticism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - defending outrageous behavior with hyper-specific medieval history knowledge

jokes.text: Because I've had the Cheesy Blasters for three days.
jokes.setup: Liz blaming various people for problems
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's still sick from fake food from opening
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing consequences of opening scene's fake food still affecting Liz

jokes.text: I am a big, 'ol liar.
jokes.setup: Jack finally giving Kenneth what he demanded
jokes.punchline: Corporate executive reduced to childish admission
jokes.explanation: Satisfying callback payoff - powerful executive humbled into childish language

jokes.text: A piece of paper that I can't really tell you about! Was this strike just over a personal thing between you and Mr. Donaghy? Massage it, Kenneth. No, it wasn't!
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining strike victory
jokes.punchline: Obvious lie about personal nature while using Jack's earlier euphemism
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Kenneth adopting Jack's euphemistic language while obviously lying

jokes.text: ♪ So put down your meth and slip on your whites ♪
jokes.setup: Jenna performs country tennis theme song
jokes.punchline: Lyrics reference meth use in heartland America
jokes.explanation: Dark country music parody. References real social issues while maintaining tennis theme.
